title: apium graveolens f. lusitanicum
intro: Apium graveolens f. lusitanicum, also known as Lusitanian wild celery or Lusitanian wild dill, is a subspecies of the wild celery plant, Apium graveolens. This plant is native to the Iberian Peninsula and is valued for its medicinal properties, including its antispasmodic, carminative, and diuretic effects. The health benefits of Apium graveolens f. lusitanicum include its ability to relieve digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and lower blood pressure, making it a popular herbal remedy for various health conditions. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
